target.title: fix(mcp): validate plugin tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardens the standalone plugin tools MCP bridge so `tools/list` no longer reads plugin `parameters` through brittle object operators.
- Materializes plugin MCP tool descriptors through the existing runtime schema projection, then validates the listed descriptor against the MCP SDK `ToolSchema` contract before exposing it.
- Quarantines incompatible plugin tools from both `listTools` and `callTool`, preserving healthy sibling tools instead of letting one bad schema poison the bridge.
- Intentionally scoped to the standalone plugin tools MCP bridge; broader agent/runtime projection behavior is unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Requested by maintainer fuzzing of unsupported runtime/tool schema surfaces.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: a plugin tool whose schema proxy throws from an object-operator trap could crash standalone plugin MCP `tools/list`; unsupported or MCP-invalid schemas could also reach the MCP client surface.
- Real environment tested: local macOS source worktree for focused repro/tests; AWS Crabbox Linux `c7a.8xlarge` for `pnpm check:changed`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... createPluginToolsMcpHandlers([...proxy parameters...]).listTools() ... EOF`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: direct repro printed `{"tools":[{"name":"neutral_probe","description":"neutral fixture tool","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{}}}]}` instead of `schema has trap`; focused Vitest passed 11 tests; AWS Crabbox run `run_402eeadac372`, lease `cbx_3620011b36ee`, exited 0 and stopped cleanly.
- Observed result after fix: proxy-backed valid schemas list successfully, implicit object roots are normalized to MCP-valid `type: "object"`, MCP-invalid `properties` maps are quarantined, `$dynamicRef` runtime-incompatible schemas are quarantined, and healthy sibling tools remain listed/callable.
- What was not tested: a live external plugin process over stdio; this patch is covered at the handler level and by the changed core/type/lint gate.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable locally due `not authenticated`; an Azure Crabbox attempt timed out before allocation (`run_44d5738f9856`, late-id hint `cbx_51573b3230fd` not present in the later Azure list), so the broad changed gate used direct AWS Crabbox instead.
- Before evidence: the pre-fix direct repro threw `schema has trap` from `tools/list` when `parameters` was a proxy with a throwing `has` trap for `type`.

## Tests and validation

- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... createPluginToolsMcpHandlers([...proxy parameters...]).listTools() ... EOF`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` after fixes: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox `run_402eeadac372` / `cbx_3620011b36ee`: `pnpm check:changed` passed

Regression coverage added for proxy-safe schema listing, implicit object-root normalization, MCP `ToolSchema` contract quarantine, and runtime-incompatible dynamic schema quarantine.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Standalone plugin MCP clients will no longer see tools with unsupported or MCP-invalid input schemas.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly: tool execution routing now only includes tools that were valid enough to list through the MCP bridge.

What is the highest-risk area?

A plugin with a malformed schema that previously appeared in `tools/list` will now be omitted until the plugin fixes its schema.

How is that risk mitigated?

Invalid tools are logged as quarantined, healthy siblings remain available, and the same materialized tool set is used for both listing and calling so clients do not see a tool that the bridge cannot safely represent.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI. No author-side blocker remains.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed before PR creation: missing MCP root `type` normalization and MCP-invalid `properties` validation.

target.title: [codex] Group sidebar recent sessions by channel
target.body:
## Summary

- Group the sidebar's existing five most recent sessions by resolved channel.
- Resolve the channel from session row metadata first, then fall back to session key parsing for channel-backed sessions, including account-scoped direct session keys.
- Add sidebar group styling and regression coverage for the top-five-before-grouping behavior.

## Why

The sidebar recent list is used as a quick navigation surface. Grouping only the already-selected five most recent sessions keeps it compact while making recent conversations easier to scan by source, without trying to surface every channel.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts"`
- `git diff --check -- ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/types.ts ui/src/styles/layout.css ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the sidebar recent list now groups the already-filtered top five sessions by channel, including account-scoped direct keys such as `agent:main:feishu:main:direct:<peer>`.
Real environment tested: Tencent Cloud OpenClaw Gateway `http://106.54.23.243:18789` running `openclaw@2026.5.31-beta.3`, connected from this PR branch's local patched Control UI dev server at `http://127.0.0.1:5173/sessions`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: temporarily added `http://127.0.0.1:5173` to `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins`, restarted the Tencent Cloud gateway, opened the local patched Control UI with the Tencent Cloud Gateway URL, confirmed the Gateway URL prompt, waited for the Sessions view to load, and captured the rendered sidebar.
Evidence after fix: screenshots captured from the real Tencent Cloud Gateway session list at local artifacts `artifacts/sidebar-recent-channel-groups-proof.png` and `artifacts/sidebar-recent-channel-groups-proof-after-parser-fix.png`; copied live browser automation output from the parser-fix run:

```text
REAL_OPENCLAW_SETUP=http://106.54.23.243:18789 gateway + local patched UI http://127.0.0.1:5173/sessions
SIDEBAR_RECENT_GROUPS=[{"channel":"feishu","label":"Feishu","sessions":["饶宇"]},{"channel":"openclaw-weixin","label":"Openclaw Weixin","sessions":["o9cq80zwIKGaDsfYCG0zOli4OSQw@im.wechat"]}]
```

Observed result after fix: the sidebar Recent section rendered grouped channel headings for the real top recent sessions; account-scoped Feishu direct sessions resolved under Feishu instead of Other.
What was not tested: production deployment of this branch's bundled Control UI assets was not installed on the Tencent Cloud host; the proof used the local patched UI dev server connected to the real Tencent Cloud Gateway data.

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Preserve `fs.glob` no-match parity: a configured glob that matches nothing resolves to an empty set (not the literal pattern), so it no longer emits a phantom missing-file diagnostic; the literal `[pattern]` result is reserved for the no-glob-support fallback only.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`** / **`src/hooks/internal-hook-dispatch.ts`**
- Keep the public `triggerInternalHook(event)` signature (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/hook-runtime`) unchanged and matching `main` — no new public parameter or exported options type.
- Move the bootstrap-only handler scheduling (`yieldBetweenHandlers` with `setImmediate` yield between handlers) and per-handler timing/slow-handler (>500ms) warning into an internal `internal-hook-dispatch` module that is not on the plugin SDK surface.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable yielding between handlers for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers (via the internal dispatch entrypoint)

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Keep the public `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` params (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`) callback-free and matching `main`; route embedded-runner substage timing through an internal `resolveBootstrapFilesForRunWithTiming` variant kept off the SDK surface.
- Support the new async workspace file loading interface.

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning exercised through the internal dispatch entrypoint
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — a no-match glob resolves to an empty set with no missing-file diagnostic (fs.glob parity)

The public plugin SDK surface is unchanged: `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` exits 0 with no new package-exported entrypoints. All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Seven coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

### 6. Restore unconfigured connection-error retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`)
Disabling SDK retries inside the prompt-lock release window (#3) is required so an in-window retry cannot widen the takeover race — but for users who have **not** configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, it also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (`ECONNRESET`, `socket hang up`, `Connection error.`) that carry no leading HTTP status. This is restored at the orchestrator layer, where the existing one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` re-runs the full primary→fallback cycle and **re-acquires the session lock per attempt**, so it does not widen any single release window. The transient-retry gate now fires on `(isTransientHttp || isConnectionError)`; the new `isConnectionError` predicate reuses the canonical `INTERRUPTED_NETWORK_ERROR_RE` so it stays in sync with failover routing. In-window `maxRetries:0` is unchanged. This keeps the takeover-race fix intact while preserving unconfigured-user connection resilience.

### 7. Restore unconfigured request-timeout retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`)
The orchestrator transient-retry restore in #6 left a symmetric gap: in addition to bare connection errors, the OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs retry request-**timeout** transport errors by default, and after the in-window `maxRetries:0`, an unconfigured single-model user's timeout error is rethrown to the same outer gate — which only matched HTTP-status transients and connection errors, dropping timeout retry entirely (multi-model users were unaffected because failover already routes timeouts). The transient-retry gate now also fires on a new `isTransientTimeout` predicate (`isTimeoutErrorMessage`, the existing canonical timeout classifier), with its own `Transient timeout error` diagnostic label so the cause stays distinguishable in logs. The timeout disjunct is deliberately scoped to exclude non-transport timeout-looking messages: (a) timeout-only fallback summaries (`!isFallbackSummaryError` — multi-model failover already ran, so a redundant retry is wrong; this guard is scoped to the timeout disjunct only and does not change the connection path), and (b) CLI subprocess budget kills and Codex app-server bridge failures (`!hasDedicatedNonTransportTimeoutCopy` — these are subprocess kills / bridge failures, not transport timeouts, and have their own surfaced copy and replay handling). The one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` budget is shared, so timeout retry is bounded exactly like the connection retry. The connection-retry path semantics from #6 are unchanged. The timeout disjunct additionally excludes local non-provider runtime coordination errors (e.g. `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`, whose message reads as `session file locked (timeout ...ms)`) via the canonical `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classifier: retrying any model would hit the same local condition, so these abort the fallback chain rather than re-run as a transport timeout.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `c21d0bc1a2` for the new timeout-retry exclusion (session write-lock timeouts excluded from the transient-timeout gate) and the restored organic-takeover real test. Supersedes the earlier blocks below.**

- **Behavior addressed**: The transient-timeout retry restore (Fix #7) used `isTimeoutErrorMessage`, which also matches local `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` messages (`session file locked (timeout ...ms): ...`). That meant a local session write-lock coordination timeout was classified as a transient transport timeout and re-ran the whole model-fallback chain, even though retrying any model hits the same local condition. The timeout disjunct now excludes non-provider runtime coordination errors via the canonical `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classifier — the same seam `model-fallback.ts` already uses to abort the chain on coordination errors. The connection/HTTP disjuncts are unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `c21d0bc1a2`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, deps installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- **Exact steps run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>`: the new session-write-lock-timeout exclusion regression in `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the organic real-setup session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence). The real-setup test depends on the `embedded-agent-helpers` mock exporting `isConnectionError`/`isTimeoutErrorMessage` (called eagerly on the catch path); those exports were missing, so this run also restores them so the test imports and runs to its assertion.
- **Observed result**: a session-write-lock-shaped timeout error now terminates as `run_failed` and is NOT re-run through the fallback chain (`runWithModelFallback` called exactly once); the organic takeover still surfaces cause-neutral resend guidance. The exclusion is proven non-tautological: removing the `!isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err)` clause makes the regression fail with the mock called twice.
- **Evidence after fix** (current-head terminal output):
```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (current head c21d0bc1a2) ===
Environment: Darwin arm64, Node v24.10.0
Commit: c21d0bc1a2c8cf7bb535dafb00299b33f9857498

--- A: Session write-lock timeout must NOT consume a transient-timeout retry (regression for the new exclusion) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > does not retry a session write-lock timeout through the transient timeout gate (#87180)
   asserts runWithModelFallback called exactly once + terminal run_failed (NOT re-run through the fallback chain)
   (proven non-tautological: removing the !isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err) clause makes this test fail with "called 2 times")
 Test Files  1 passed (1) ; Tests  228 passed (228)

--- B: Organic real-setup session-takeover proof (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  316ms
   (this real test now imports and runs to its assertion after restoring the missing isConnectionError/isTimeoutErrorMessage mock exports it depends on)
 Test Files  1 passed (1) ; Tests  1 passed (1)

--- C: Code-path verification at current head ---
Timeout disjunct now excludes local non-provider runtime coordination errors (e.g. session write-lock timeouts):
3232:        !isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err);
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ c21d0bc1a2 ===
```
- **What was not tested**: The exact live Feishu/DeepSeek production timing race is not replayed end-to-end; the strongest proof remains the deterministic local HTTP + on-disk session takeover coverage plus the session-write-lock-timeout exclusion regression above.

### Earlier proof (head `e0ec982bb8`)


- **Behavior addressed**: For users who have not configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, the in-window `maxRetries: 0` (required so an in-window SDK retry cannot widen the takeover race) previously also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (ECONNRESET, socket hang up, `Connection error.`). That resilience is restored at the orchestrator layer, where each retry re-acquires the session lock and does not widen any single release window. The in-window `maxRetries: 0` race fix is unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0dc5a3010a`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, deps installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- **Exact steps run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node_modules/.bin/vitest run <suite>`: the orchestrator connection-error retry cases (one-retry-then-success and bounded-loop), the HTTP transient 521 diagnostic case (the previously-red shard, now green with the preserved wording), the `isConnectionError` predicate unit coverage, the organic real-setup session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), and the in-window session-lock suite; plus `tsgo:core`/`tsgo:core:test` typecheck and oxlint on the changed files.

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.


</details>

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter
- Request-timeout transport error triggers exactly one orchestrator retry then succeeds, and a persistent timeout is bounded to a single retry (no infinite loop)
- CLI subprocess timeouts and Codex app-server bridge failures are NOT retried through the transient gate and still surface their dedicated copy
- A timeout-only fallback summary is not retried through the transient timeout disjunct (no full primary→fallback re-run)

target.title: fix(ui): keep session switching responsive
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep chat/session switching responsive by coalescing explicit session-switch reloads without caching event-driven `commands.list` results.
- Harden lifecycle recovery so internal webchat late errors can recover persisted successful output without masking external delivery failures.
- Fix Control UI gateway auth so cached device-token paths wait for a signed challenge nonce, including retry after shared-token mismatch.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/commands.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-expansion-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts --format=unix`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`
- `node openclaw.mjs --version`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Control UI session switches avoid redundant reload stalls while preserving fresh event-driven state, and gateway/device-token auth no longer sends cached device tokens without the signed nonce payload.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using targeted gateway/UI Vitest files, oxlint, autoreview, and the OpenClaw CLI entrypoint.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/commands.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-expansion-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`; `node openclaw.mjs --version`.
Evidence after fix:
```text
$ node openclaw.mjs --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (085dae8)
$ .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```
Observed result after fix: targeted lifecycle, chat/session, and browser gateway auth tests pass; autoreview found no accepted/actionable findings after the nonce and lifecycle-boundary fixes.
What was not tested: full browser E2E interaction against a running gateway was not run; coverage is focused unit/integration proof for the changed surfaces.

target.title: feat(agents): auto-trim lean local model tools
target.body:
## Summary

- add `localModelLean: "auto"` so small resolved model context caps trim heavyweight browser, cron, and message tools without forcing lean mode on larger models
- route the effective model context cap through embedded runs, Codex dynamic tools, Codex side-question tools, and `tools.effective` inventory so advertised tools match runtime behavior
- handle provider/model/default/per-agent context cap precedence, including provider `contextWindow`, provider `contextTokens`, `agents.defaults.contextTokens`, and `agents.list[].contextTokens`
- update schema/help/docs and Plugin SDK API baseline for the new config value

Related to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599, but does not close it. This PR addresses the small/local model tool-surface pressure lane.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.model-provider-collision.test.ts src/agents/context-window-guard.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/setup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed on head `b25913130f0`, 4 shards / 262 tests.
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check` - passed.
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check` - passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md` - passed.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md` - passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` - passed on touched TS files.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` - passed on touched TS files.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean on head `b25913130f0`, no accepted/actionable findings, overall `0.82`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4cad88e21b94`, slug `crimson-prawn`, run `run_1ba6ba05d96f`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, `docs`, exit `0`, cleanup `leaseStopped=true`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `localModelLean: "auto"` trims heavyweight default tools when the effective model context cap is 64K tokens or smaller, while preserving larger tool surfaces when configured provider or per-agent caps resolve above that threshold.
Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/docs/format/lint/baseline checks, structured branch review, and AWS Crabbox Linux `pnpm check:changed`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: see Verification.
Evidence after fix: deterministic tests cover auto threshold behavior, provider context precedence, default/per-agent cap precedence, embedded run budget resolution, Codex dynamic tools, Codex side-question tools, and `tools.effective` inventory projection.
Observed result after fix: focused local proof and AWS Crabbox changed gate both pass on head `b25913130f0`.
What was not tested: no new live Ollama/provider runtime call was run in this refresh; this PR is verified through deterministic config/runtime tests plus the remote changed gate.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): agent tools for group create / add participants / invite link
target.body:
## What

Adds three WhatsApp agent tools so an agent can manage groups programmatically, gated behind a new `channels.whatsapp.actions.groups` flag (default `false`):

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `whatsapp_group_create` | Create a group with a subject + E.164 participants. Returns the new group JID. |
| `whatsapp_group_add_participants` | Add E.164 participants to an existing group JID. |
| `whatsapp_group_invite_link` | Resolve the invite code + `https://chat.whatsapp.com/...` link for a group the bot administers. |

## Why

WhatsApp already supports outbound sends (`openclaw message send`) and the `react` action, but there's no first-class way for an agent to create groups or manage membership. Baileys exposes `groupCreate` / `groupParticipantsUpdate` / `groupInviteCode` on the socket; this surfaces them as proper agent tools.

## How

- **`ActiveWebListener`** gains `createGroup` / `addGroupParticipants` / `getGroupInviteCode`, implemented in `createWebSendApi` over the reconnect-aware socket (same path as `sendMessage`/`sendPoll`). Phone numbers are normalized to JIDs with the existing `toWhatsappJid` helper.
- **`createWhatsAppGroupTools({ cfg })`** registers the tools via the channel's `agentTools` factory — they only appear when the gate is enabled, and each re-checks the gate (via live `getRuntimeConfig()`) and requires an active linked listener at execute time, so a disabled or unlinked install can't invoke them.
- **Security:** `actions.groups` defaults to `false` because these actions can add arbitrary people to chats — opt-in, consistent with the existing `reactions`/`polls` gates.

## Config

```json5
{ channels: { whatsapp: { actions: { groups: true } } } }
```

## Tests / checks

- Unit tests for the listener group methods (`send-api.test.ts`) and the agent-tool wrapper (`agent-tools-groups.test.ts`: gate visibility + enforcement, JID/participant validation, not-linked error).
- `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test` clean.
- Regenerated `bundled-channel-config-metadata` (`config:channels:gen`); `check:bundled-channel-config-metadata` passes.
- Updated `docs/channels/whatsapp.md`.

target.title: fix(plugins): guard plugin tool descriptor accessors
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat unreadable plugin tool required fields as malformed before wrapping, caching, or exposing the tool.
- Keep valid sibling plugin tools available when one tool has hostile `name`, `execute`, `description`, or descriptor-cache accessors.
- Make effective tool inventory tolerate unreadable optional display metadata and fall back to raw descriptions instead of crashing doctor/inventory paths.

Related hardening context:

- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88994
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89013
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89016

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: A plugin tool with hostile descriptor accessors could crash plugin tool resolution, descriptor caching, or effective tool inventory after earlier shape checks, causing healthy sibling tools or doctor/inventory output to disappear.

Real environment tested: Local focused regression tests plus AWS Crabbox changed-gate proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory-build.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory-build.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Focused Vitest passed: 21 agent inventory tests and 79 plugin tests.
- Static checks passed: oxlint, oxfmt, and `git diff --check`.
- Direct repros changed from throwing hostile descriptor/display metadata getters to returning safe descriptors or inventory fallbacks.
- Autoreview: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- Crabbox: provider `aws`, run `run_2f40fa77f3e2`, lease `cbx_cc78e0a83171`, lanes `core, coreTests`, exit `0`.

Observed result after fix: Malformed plugin tools with unreadable required fields are isolated with diagnostics, valid siblings continue loading, optional descriptor/display metadata no longer crashes descriptor caching or inventory, and required descriptor-cache fields cannot be cached as empty fallbacks.

What was not tested: Blacksmith Testbox could not be used because the local Blacksmith CLI was not authenticated, so the broad changed gate ran through AWS Crabbox instead.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): extract full HTML body content
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #82685 by treating title-only Readability output as failed body extraction instead of returning only the page title.
- Normalizes title-only comparison through plain-text markdown stripping, so markdown-mode headings like `# Sample Page` also fall through instead of being returned as body content.
- Preserves the configured-provider-before-raw-HTML order for title-only Readability pages, so existing provider routing remains the first recovery path when configured.
- Hardens the runtime body scanner so fake `<body>` tags inside comments, raw-text containers, and inert/nested `<template>` regions do not replace the real visible document body.
- Keeps the change scoped to the `web_fetch` runtime extraction path; shared HTML-to-markdown helpers, provider configuration, network policy, and public API shape are unchanged.

What problem does this PR solve?

`web_fetch` could accept Readability output that only contained the page title as successful body extraction. In default markdown mode, that included heading-only output such as `# Sample Page`, because the title comparison did not strip markdown before deciding whether extraction only returned the title. In that state, users and agents could receive only the title instead of visible page content. The runtime body scanner also needed to preserve the document-body boundary while skipping HTML regions that can legally contain literal fake body markup but are not the visible body.

Why does this matter now?

The linked issue is user-visible: downstream tool output is misleading when `web_fetch` returns title-only text or hidden/inert fake body content instead of the actual page body.

What is the intended outcome?

This is a root-cause extraction-state fix: title-only Readability output no longer completes extraction, and body-boundary scanning now enforces the browser-visible body invariant before raw HTML text is produced. Successful behavior is provider output when a configured provider handles the title-only page, or visible body text from local raw HTML cleanup when no provider output is available.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change shared `htmlToMarkdown`, provider configuration, network/auth/security policy, migrations, schema, public API shape, or the provider catalog. It only changes the `web_fetch` runtime extraction decision and focused regression coverage for that path.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the source-of-truth boundary between local Readability extraction, configured provider routing, and runtime-local raw HTML body cleanup. The provider compatibility boundary is preserved: title-only pages still try configured provider routing before local raw HTML cleanup, while scanner hardening only affects the local cleanup path when it is reached.

## Linked context

- Closes #82685.
- Updates PR #89028 after review feedback called out missing real-behavior proof, raw-text/template scanner gaps, and provider compatibility risk.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `web_fetch` no longer returns only the page title when Readability emits title-only text; it continues extraction and returns visible body content when no configured provider output is available.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw `web_fetch` tool execution with the real Readability extractor and a deterministic HTML fetch fixture. External network access was not required for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a Node proof script that calls the real Readability extractor, then calls `createWebFetchTool().execute` with `{ extractMode: 'markdown' }` against the same title-only fixture with provider fetching disabled.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  web_fetch title-only fallback proof
  fixture url: https://fixture.openclaw.test/title-only-template-raw-text
  readability.title: "Sample Page"
  readability.text: "Sample Page"
  readability.titleOnly: true
  web_fetch.status: 200
  web_fetch.contentType: text/html
  web_fetch.extractor: raw-html
  web_fetch.title.hasSamplePage: true
  web_fetch.text.hasBodyMarker: true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the fixture forced title-only Readability output, and with no configured provider output available, the `web_fetch` result used `raw-html` with the expected body marker present.
- What was not tested: the exact original reporter page, because the issue did not include a concrete URL or full HTML fixture.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live proof uses a deterministic fixture so the title-only condition and expected body marker are reproducible. The configured-provider order is covered by a focused regression test that asserts provider output wins before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new focused regressions failed before this fix: the nested-template raw-text case surfaced fake script body text, and the provider-order guard caught the intermediate regression where local raw HTML cleanup ran before the configured provider.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "falls back to body HTML when markdown readability returns only the page title heading"` — failed before the latest repair with `expected 'readability' to be 'raw-html'`, then passed after markdown-stripping title comparison.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "tries provider fallback before body HTML when readability returns only the page title"` — passed; configured provider output still wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` — passed, 32 tests.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added markdown-mode title-only coverage where Readability returns `# Sample Page`, proving heading-only output is treated as incomplete extraction and falls through to visible body HTML when no provider output is available.
- Added coverage for fake body tags inside raw-text content nested within inert templates, proving the scanner returns the real body marker and excludes the fake body text.
- Restored title-only/provider coverage so configured provider output is used before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The markdown-heading title-only regression initially returned the `readability` result `# Sample Page` instead of continuing to `raw-html` body content.
- The nested-template raw-text regression initially returned fake script body text instead of the real body marker.
- The provider-order guard caught an intermediate regression where recoverable local body HTML bypassed the configured provider.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added/updated for the scanner gap and provider compatibility boundary.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. For title-only Readability pages, `web_fetch` now continues extraction instead of stopping at the title. Configured provider order is preserved, and local raw HTML body cleanup is used only when provider output is unavailable.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No expansion of capability. The change stays inside `web_fetch` extraction and does not broaden network access, auth, secrets, or tool execution.

What is the highest-risk area?

Compatibility for title-only pages: `web_fetch` now treats title-only Readability as incomplete and may proceed to configured provider routing or local raw HTML cleanup instead of returning only the title.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The canonical source of truth remains `web_fetch` runtime extraction: Readability first, configured provider routing second, local raw HTML cleanup last for HTML extraction failures.
- The change is limited to title-only Readability output after plain-text markdown normalization; normal successful Readability output is unchanged.
- The focused provider regression asserts that configured provider output still wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- Raw HTML cleanup stays runtime-local to `web_fetch`; shared conversion helpers and public API shape are unchanged.
- Related open PR scan result: PR #89028 is the active repair path for #82685, and current main did not already contain this title-only/body-boundary fix when this update started.
- Patch-quality note: the added `null`/`undefined` parser results are explicit scanner miss signals for malformed/non-tag input, not symptom masking; they allow scanning to continue until the real body boundary is found or no body is present.
- Size note: the diff is confined to one runtime file and its focused tests; the line count is mostly regression coverage for existing review findings in this PR.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the updated branch is submitted and CI settles.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer: review the title-only extraction behavior with provider-first compatibility preserved.
- CI: rerun on the updated PR branch after submission.
- External proof: no exact reporter URL/HTML was available, so proof uses representative deterministic HTML that reproduces the title-only/body-cleanup invariant.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 fixed: the author-side code/test changes for ClawSweeper's waiting-on-author review have been applied; after submission, ClawSweeper can re-evaluate the status label.
- RF-002/RF-004/RF-008/RF-009 fixed: title-only comparison now strips markdown through plain-text normalization before comparing Readability output with the page title.
- RF-003/RF-007 stale/current-state addressed: the previously reported `checks-node-compact-small-whole-2` failure is no longer visible on the latest collected check rollup, and CI will rerun on the updated PR branch after submission.
- RF-005 addressed as maintainer decision context: provider-first order remains intentional and documented as the compatibility/cost tradeoff reviewers should evaluate.
- RF-006 addressed: the PR body states that the exact reporter URL/HTML was unavailable and the proof uses a deterministic representative fixture.
- RF-010 fixed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` passed locally, 32 tests.
- RF-011 fixed: the provider-before-body fallback order regression passed after the markdown-title repair.
- RF-012 fixed: the new markdown-heading title-only regression failed before the repair with `expected 'readability' to be 'raw-html'` and passed after the repair.

target.title: fix(codex): before_prompt_build receives conversation messages
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore Codex `before_prompt_build` hook input so plugins can observe loaded OpenClaw session messages again.
- Keep Codex app-server model input and `llm_input` diagnostics on the native thread path without mirrored OpenClaw history.
- Copy hook-observation messages per prompt-build pass so hook-side mutation cannot leak into a later prompt rebuild.

Fixes #88353

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex `before_prompt_build` no longer receives an empty `messages` array after session history has been loaded; Codex model input and `llm_input.historyMessages` still avoid mirrored OpenClaw history.
- **Real environment tested:** Local PR worktree after syncing `origin/main`, at HEAD `7758087aa855bf1c602484b34848048d625aa32c`, with installed dependencies and local `codex-cli 0.133.0` available as the Codex app-server command. The proof script imported the current worktree code and ran the real Codex app-server turn-construction path through the stdio app-server command.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```text
  source /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-89004-evidence/context.env
  cd "$TASK_WORKTREE"
  PROOF_HEAD="$(git rev-parse HEAD)" node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/codex-app-server-hook-proof.mjs" 2>&1 | tee "$EVIDENCE_DIR/codex-app-server-hook-proof-after-sync.log"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "applies before_prompt_build|keeps thread-start developer instructions stable|routes Codex model input diagnostics"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "before_prompt_build|llm_input|context engine|context-engine"
  OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/boundary-file-read.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/infra/hardlink-guards.test.ts src/infra/replace-file.test.ts src/infra/resolve-system-bin.test.ts src/infra/safe-package-install.test.ts src/infra/stable-node-path.test.ts src/infra/watch-node.test.ts --run
  pnpm exec oxfmt --threads=1 --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** The real app-server hook proof wrote `codex-app-server-hook-proof-output.json` and `codex-app-server-hook-proof-after-sync.log`. Terminal output excerpt:
  ```json
  {
    "hookEvents": [
      {
        "messagesLength": 1,
        "roles": ["assistant"],
        "text": "previous transcript marker CODEX-HOOK-PROOF-B2FAEC50"
      }
    ],
    "llmInputEvents": [
      {
        "promptIncludesHookContext": true,
        "historyMessagesLength": 0,
        "historyContainsPreviousTurn": false
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` now supplies a fresh copy of loaded `historyMessages` to `before_prompt_build`, so plugins observe the previous assistant turn. The Codex model-input and `llm_input` paths still read from the empty Codex-native history array. The new regression assertion also verifies a hook-side mutation from the first prompt-build pass is absent from the later prompt rebuild.
- **What was not tested:** The real app-server proof validated the hook/model-input boundary during turn construction, but the downstream Codex turn timed out waiting for completion in this environment after the proof point was captured. A full repository suite and a full successful live Codex assistant answer were not run.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix.

## CI failure attribution

- The visible failing current-head CI check was `checks-node-core-runtime-infra-file-safety` on PR head `efd74746717cefb83afa2b01c49f64cde1f92414`; its log showed Vitest produced no output for 900000ms and was terminated with exit code 143, not a failed assertion in this Codex patch.
- After syncing `origin/main`, the matching local infra file-safety target passed on HEAD `7758087aa855bf1c602484b34848048d625aa32c`: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 39 passed (39)`.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: The real proof still validates the changed hook/model-input boundary rather than a full downstream assistant answer. The downstream Codex turn timed out after the proof point was captured in this environment, so this remains disclosed under “What was not tested”; the changed behavior is covered by real app-server proof plus focused and adjacent regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the touched contract because the production change remains narrowly scoped to the Codex app-server hook/model-input boundary, and the new assertion covers the reviewer-suggested mutation isolation case.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** History-sensitive plugins using `before_prompt_build.messages` can again distinguish a resumed Codex session from a brand-new session, without re-injecting mirrored OpenClaw transcript history into the native Codex model input.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change Codex `turn/start` model input history, `llm_input.historyMessages`, provider routing, plugin APIs, generated protocol/schema files, dependencies, workflows, lockfiles, or any non-Codex extension behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth stays split by owner: loaded OpenClaw `historyMessages` are only the hook-observation input, while Codex native thread continuity remains owned by the app-server thread and current `turn/start.input`; the proof and tests verify that the model-consumed history path stays empty.
- **Patch quality notes:** The patch avoids fallback behavior and keeps the two channels explicit: hook observation gets copied loaded session messages, while Codex model input and diagnostics keep the empty native-thread history. No dependencies, generated files, workflows, lockfiles, or cross-extension public APIs were changed.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** A session with a previous assistant message calls `before_prompt_build`; the hook sees that previous message, while the Codex turn input still contains only the current prompt/context and diagnostics keep history empty.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The focused regression locks the broken public hook surface and the adjacent mutation-isolation case without asserting unrelated Codex runtime implementation details or broad session replay behavior.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, session-state, plugin-contract.
- **Risk explanation:** The touched surface is in the Codex app-server prompt-build path, so a mistake could affect Codex turn construction or plugin prompt shaping for resumed sessions.
- **Why acceptable:** The production diff only changes the input passed to `before_prompt_build`; the Codex model-input history array remains empty, `llm_input.historyMessages` remains empty in proof, focused/adjacent tests passed, and the matching local infra file-safety shard passed after syncing main.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The Codex app-server path reused the empty `codexModelInputHistoryMessages` array for `before_prompt_build`. That array exists to keep mirrored OpenClaw transcript history out of Codex native model input, but the hook contract is separate and should observe the already-loaded OpenClaw session messages.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch fixes the source contract invariant instead of masking the symptom: hook observation and Codex model input are separate channels, so `before_prompt_build` receives loaded session messages while Codex model input and `llm_input` diagnostics continue to use the empty Codex-native history array. The hook input is now copied per prompt-build pass so plugin mutation cannot alter later prompt construction.

## Verification

- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "applies before_prompt_build|keeps thread-start developer instructions stable|routes Codex model input diagnostics"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "before_prompt_build|llm_input|context engine|context-engine"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --threads=1 --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(browser): read Windows Chrome version from build dir in doctor
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw doctor` on Windows reports `Could not determine the installed Chrome version. Chrome MCP requires Google Chrome 144+ on this host.` even when a supported Chrome (e.g. 148.0.7778.179) is installed at the standard path and the browser tool itself connects and works fine. See #87312.
- Root cause: `readBrowserVersion()` resolves the version by running `chrome.exe --version`. On macOS/Linux that prints the version to stdout, but on Windows the GUI binary does not attach to the parent console, so `--version` produces no stdout and detection returns `null`.
- Fix: add a `win32` branch to `readBrowserVersion()` that reads the version from the Chromium install layout (the newest sibling `Application\<version>` build directory), falling back to the executable's file metadata via PowerShell `ProductVersion` for non-standard/portable layouts. This mirrors the existing `darwin` branch, which already reads the app bundle `Info.plist` instead of `--version`.
- Out of scope: the minimum-version policy and the macOS/Linux code paths are unchanged.
- Success: on Windows, doctor detects Chrome 144+ and stops emitting the false warning; other platforms behave exactly as before.
- Reviewer focus: the new `readWindowsBrowserVersion()` helper and the `win32` branch in `readBrowserVersion()` in `extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.executables.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #87312

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No; community bug report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: doctor false positive "Could not determine the installed Chrome version" on Windows with a working Chrome install.
- Real environment tested: macOS (development host). The Windows-specific path was exercised by unit tests, **not** on a live Windows host (I do not have a Windows machine available).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo`, `oxlint`, `oxfmt --check` on the touched files
- Evidence after fix:
  - New `on Windows` unit tests in `chrome.version.test.ts` stub `process.platform = "win32"` and build a real temporary directory mirroring the `Application\<version>` install layout. They assert that `readBrowserVersion()`:
    - derives the newest version from sibling build directories using numeric (not lexical) ordering, and does **not** spawn PowerShell when a version directory exists;
    - falls back to `powershell.exe ... (Get-Item -LiteralPath '<exe>').VersionInfo.ProductVersion` when no version directory exists;
    - returns `null` when neither signal is available.
  - Results: `chrome.version.test.ts` 6 passed; `doctor-browser.test.ts` 11 passed; `tsgo` 0 errors; `oxlint` 0 errors; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Observed result after fix: on the simulated Windows layout `readBrowserVersion()` returns e.g. `148.0.7778.179`, so `parseBrowserMajorVersion()` yields `148 >= 144` and doctor would not emit the warning.
- What was not tested: a live Windows host with a real Chrome install. The PowerShell command was not executed against a real `chrome.exe`; the fallback is covered via a mocked `execFileSync`.
- Proof limitations: I cannot run Windows locally, so the version-directory layout and the PowerShell `ProductVersion` fallback are validated by unit tests and the well-documented Chromium-on-Windows `--version` behavior rather than a live run. Live confirmation on a Windows host would strengthen this PR; happy to adjust if a maintainer can reproduce.

target.title: fix: route Discord CLI sends through gateway
target.body:
Direct `openclaw message send --channel discord` currently takes the Discord outbound adapter's direct path, which bypasses gateway-scoped message_sending/message_sent hooks. That leaves runtime ownership guards such as Action Gate unable to enforce protected Discord scopes for local CLI sends.

This changes the Discord outbound adapter to advertise `deliveryMode: "gateway"`, so core direct CLI/core sends route through the gateway delivery path. The gateway handler still performs the final platform send through the adapter, avoiding a send loop while ensuring gateway host hooks run.

Tests:
- `pnpm --filter extension-discord test -- extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `pnpm build:strict`
